handoff_packets.content:
{
  "cwd": "/Users/sam.zhang/Documents/codex-supervisor",
  "execution_layer_policy": {
    "agentic_lead_policy": {
      "min_subagents": 0,
      "policy": "off",
      "required_evidence_grade": "self_reported",
      "required_roles": [],
      "solo_exception_for_artifact_only_gates": false
    },
    "allowed_dynamic_workflow_task_classes": [],
    "codex_supervises_final_artifact": true,
    "dynamic_workflow_task_class": null,
    "lead_execution_layer": "single_lead_worker",
    "mode": "lead_direct",
    "preview_required_gates": [],
    "supervision_layer": "codex_plus_lead"
  },
  "expected_decisions": [],
  "expected_objections": [],
  "expected_specialists": [],
  "gate": "tdd_review",
  "instruction": "Supervisor-owned workflow gate: tdd_review.\n\nIntent:\nMake the Cursor independent reviewer path either return a valid typed outcome or fail with a deterministic, recoverable, typed artifact, so a reviewer that ran but produced no parseable verdict is classified as an infrastructure failure with a documented recovery path instead of hard-blocking the gate in limbo indistinguishable from a quality rejection. EXACT PROBLEM: invoke_cursor_agent in supervisor/cursor_agent.py calls evaluate_outcome_fidelity. When Cursor output lacks a parseable <dual_agent_outcome> block, fidelity fails with missing dual_agent_outcome, then is recorded as cursor_review_failed and the gate blocks. The gate cannot distinguish Cursor returned REVISE or DENY from Cursor never returned a valid verdict. Scope: add bounded corrective retries in cursor_agent.py; terminal failure becomes typed reviewer_infrastructure_unavailable or reviewer_contract_unmet without fabricating an outcome; classify and recover at the gate/policy layer in mcp_tools/codex_supervisor_stdio.py without counting missing verdict as accept and without bypassing gates; persist reviewer verdict/classification to ledger so Transport closed during read_gate_transcript cannot lose it. Non-goals: do not change AND semantics for real verdicts, do not auto-accept missing verdicts, do not weaken P1/P2/P3/P13/P14 or independent review, do not build lower-level MCP reconnect. Acceptance: valid Cursor typed outcome behaves normally; malformed/no outcome retries then records deterministic infrastructure artifact and classification; genuine Cursor revise/deny still blocks; simulated Transport closed during transcript read does not lose persisted reviewer verdict; no missing verdict counted as accept; suite stays green; typed outcome plus reviewer pass on this run.\n\nReview this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.\nUse decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.",
  "lead_skill": null,
  "outcome_validation_policy": {
    "fidelity_failure": "abort_to_operator",
    "malformed_outcome": "retry_once_with_corrective_packet",
    "subprocess_failure": "abort_to_operator",
    "timeout": "abort_to_operator"
  },
  "packet_schema_version": "dual-agent-handoff/v1",
  "planning_artifacts": [
    {
      "kind": "prd",
      "mutable_by_worker": false,
      "path": "docs/dual-agent/cursor-review-reliability-20260531/source/prd.md",
